title: Alyssum microphyllum
taxonomy: Alyssum microphyllum is classified in the family Brassicaceae. It belongs to the genus Alyssum and the order Brassicales. This flowering plant is further classified as a species within the kingdom Plantae. At the more specific level, it is classified as a dicotyledonous plant. Alyssum microphyllum is a type of annual or perennial plant.
uses: Alyssum microphyllum has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are used to make teas and infusions, which are believed to help alleviate colds, fever, and respiratory issues. In some cultures, Alyssum microphyllum is also used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's effectiveness for medicinal purposes. The plant's use in traditional medicine is mainly based on anecdotal evidence and has not been extensively scientifically studied.

side_effects: Alyssum microphyllum, also known as dwarf alison, is not typically associated with any major health concerns. intro: Alyssum microphyllum, also known as dwarf alyssum, is a perennial herb belonging to the Brassicaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. The plant contains bioactive constituents like isothiocyanates, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Alyssum microphyllum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to make capsules and tablets.
